label: V1.0.1 — Best/Worst → NET; Bang-for-$ → Draft Rating
description: First published version of the Draft Hub analytics model. This establishes the V1 baseline. Future tweaks bump the version and are logged here.

methodology_signature.weekly_grading: z = (score - p50_rostered_starter) / (p80 - p50); Elite z>=1.0, Plus 0.25<=z<1.0, Neutral -0.5<=z<0.25, Dud z<-0.5
methodology_signature.baseline_source: Rostered starters (weeklyresults.status='starter') joined with player_weeklyscoringresults; authoritative for pre-2020 and post-2020 alike
methodology_signature.games_played_gate: No Injury-Bust tier; small-sample rookies ride the raw NET classification. gp_3yr_total surfaces for context.
methodology_signature.draft_rating: per-pick Δ = actual 3yr NET − slot-expected 3yr NET (median at that round+slot); per-owner avg then shrunk via Bayesian prior (0-mean, 20-pick pseudo-count); scaled 0-100 anchored to league distribution
methodology_signature.pick_ownership: owner_name resolved by (season, franchise_id) → franchises table → transactions_adddrop → transactions_auction → team_name normalized lookup; legacy JSON owner field is last-resort (known mis-attribution risk)
methodology_signature.future_pick_projection: ordinal rank from 10yr reg-season AP% (owner-tracked, new-owner sample clipped) + bracket-aware playoff-Δ adjustment (bed-shitter/rally in top-6, tanker/try-hard in bot-6); brackets sealed; FINISH_TO_SLOT mapping 7→1.01, 8→1.02, 9→1.03, 10→1.04, 11→1.05, 12→1.06, 6→1.07, 5→1.08, 4→1.09, 3→1.10, 2→1.11, 1→1.12
methodology_signature.idp_window: R6 is IDP-only random drawing (May 2, 2026 6pm ET); not projected
methodology_signature.contract_parsing: MFL contractStatus='TAG' override rejected; authoritative tag = literal 'Tag' chunk in contractInfo pipe-string; 'No Further Extensions' parsed as walk-year marker

changes.summary: Align Best/Worst pick attribution with All-Play winning % (NET-based); fix metric-popup semantic lookup; add Slot Percentile; add versioning system.
details: Best Pick = highest 3yr NET (was: tier-ranked composite). NET correlates with AP% at +0.850; it's the authoritative 'impact on winning' metric.
Worst Pick = lowest 3yr NET (was: tier-ranked composite). Same reasoning.
Bang-for-$ = highest Draft Rating (was: raw points above slot-expected). Lavonte David now correctly surfaces as Keith Creelman's Bang-for-$ at 3.04 / +77.4 Draft Rating.
Cell-level popup definitions now resolve by per-cell semantic (not metric-wide). Clicking Slot-Exp NET shows Slot-Exp NET definition regardless of which view is active.
Added Slot Percentile field: 0-100 rank of pick's NET within its exact (round, slot) historical population. TRich 2012 1.01 = 0 (worst 1.01 ever). Zeke 2016 1.01 = 92.9.
Introduced VERSION.json + CHANGELOG.md versioning system with badge in hub header.
Games-weighted per-year rank averaging (was: simple average).
Tier popup + metric popup now lead with 'In plain English' block before technical definition.
Shrinkage explanation rewritten with plain-English first, technical second, plus concrete value interpretation.

changes.summary: Initial published version. Baseline methodology locked.
details: NET tier classifier: E+P − 0.5×Dud with thresholds 30/15/0
Games-weighted 3-year averaging for E+P, Dud, NET, Win Chunks, PPG ranks
Rostered-starter baselines for positional win-chunk calculations (uniform methodology 2011-2025)
Draft Rating with Bayesian shrinkage (20-pick pseudo-count) and 0-100 normalization
Future pick projection using 10yr owner-tracked AP% with bracket-aware playoff-Δ adjustments
Pre-2017 ownership inference via chronological replay of drafts + adds + drops + trades
Universal tier-click and metric-cell-click popups with plain-English explanations
